description.1: A DDoS attack targeted Group H in the CHAMPS 2026 group stage, affecting several teams including CABO-VERDE, COBRA-WERDE, and SAUDOVIA. The attack was attributed to a single IP address, but its origin and motivation remain unclear. The incident has raised concerns about the security of online sports events and the potential for future attacks on FIFA tournaments.
description.2:
A DDoS attack targeted ExtraHop's cloud-native network detection and response platform.
Who was affected: ExtraHop customers who rely on the Reveal(x) 360 platform for full network transparency.
Key technical details: The attack involved a sophisticated cyberattack that exploited vulnerabilities in the platform, allowing hackers to gain unauthorized access and disrupt service.
description.3:
A DDoS attack targeted Vogel IT-Medien GmbH's website.
B The attack was carried out by a group claiming to be affiliated with the Vogel Communications Group, which used the compromised data to send targeted advertisements and perform market research.
C The incident occurred on August 8, 2025, and affected multiple websites and online platforms owned or managed by the Vogel Communications Group.
description.4:
A DDoS attack targeted online casinos, causing widespread disruption to their mobile app market.
Casinos saw a significant rise in popularity and their market size grew as a result of technological advances.
While Lenovo's decision to come back with iOS 26.5 brings important changes in messaging, maps, security, and daily use.
description.5:
A DDoS attack targeted the MadLads NFT minted by Armani Ferrante and Tristan Yver.
Billions of requests were routed through the crypto wallet Backpack, exceeding its capacity, causing a distributed denial of service (DDoS) attack.
The attack knocked out two RPC nodes serving as access points to the Solana blockchain and the user interface on Cloudflare.
description.6: A DDoS attack targeted Microsoft 365, causing widespread disruption to enterprise communication systems. The attack was likely launched using a sophisticated AI-assisted attack tool, exploiting vulnerabilities in the software's native security filters. Microsoft has since patched the vulnerability and warned users of potential future attacks.
description.7:
A Radware Bot Manager has enhanced its features to offer advanced security for native mobile applications.
Bearing in mind that traditional security controls are limited in detecting sophisticated bots, Radware Bot Manager's advances provide integrated authentication and new identity algorithms.
Citing increasing sophistication of bot attacks, Gabi Malka notes that defending against their tactics requires proactive security.
description.8:
A Telxius has installed two new scrubbing centers in Latin America to strengthen protection against Distributed Denial-of-Service attacks on its network.
The new mitigation centers are strategically located, one on the Pacific coast, in Lurín, Peru, and the other on the Atlantic coast, in Santos, Brazil, boosting the mitigation capacity of its anti-DDoS service.
These two scrubbing centers add to Telxius’ existing mitigators in USA and Europe and take advantage of the company’s low-latency high-availability submarine cable infrastructure for traffic handling.
description.9:
A DDoS attack targeted a Dark Web forum, compromising user data and disrupting online activities.
The incident was attributed to a state-sponsored actor, who used the platform to spread malware and steal sensitive information.
Red Hot Cyber announced a new live-class on Dark Web & Cyber Threat Intelligence, offering an immersive learning experience.
description.10:
A DDoS attack targeted openSUSE Leap 15.4, causing widespread disruption.
The attack exploited a previously unknown vulnerability in the 'pkgconf' package, which is part of the openSUSE Leap 15.4 distribution.
Several security updates for affected systems were released to patch the vulnerability and prevent further attacks.
description.11:
A DDoS attack targeted several organizations in 2022, prompting hackers to adopt more professional and corporate tactics to ensure higher returns.
Ransomware remains one of the biggest cybersecurity threats that organizations and governments continue to face, with attackers using various extortion techniques to extort data from victims.
With the rise of ransomware-as-a-service, cybercriminals have made it easier for others to launch attacks, including distributed denial-of-service protection.
description.12:
A Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attack targeted Israeli internet sites, including those of universities and government institutions.
The attacks were carried out by a group called "Anonymous Sudan," which claimed responsibility for the cyberattacks as a response to Israel's policies towards the Palestinian Authority.
Key technical details included that the attacks used a DDoS method to overwhelm the websites' servers with requests.
description.13:
A DDoS attack targeted Iran's Central Bank and messaging apps.
The attack was thwarted by Iranian authorities in January, December 2022, and April last year, while a cyberattack on Iran's gas distribution network in October 2021 caused widespread disruption.
Most cyberattacks have been repelled since then, although some have been foiled successfully.
description.14:
A DNS NXDOMAIN flood DDoS attack is being targeted against healthcare organizations across the U.S., with attackers seeking to overload Domain Name System (DNS) servers with fake requests in an attempt to prevent authorized users from accessing websites or services.
Healthcare organizations have been warned by the Department of Health and Human Services' Health Sector Cybersecurity Coordination Center (HC3) that these attacks can result in higher utilization of resources on the server, leading to cache being filled up with NXDOMAIN replies.
The HC3 has recommended implementing DNS response rate limiting, ensuring a cache refresh takes place, and applying rate limiting on traffic to overwhelmed servers to mitigate risk.
description.15:
A DDoS attack targeted Battle.net servers, causing high latency and connection issues for players on Blizzard's platform.
Players reported problems logging in, including errors and disconnections, due to the server overload caused by the attack.
Blizzard confirmed that they were investigating the issue and working to mitigate it, with some players able to log back in after a brief outage.
description.16:
A suspected distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack downed several websites broadcasting President Putin's state of the nation address on Tuesday.
Reuters said journalists based in multiple locations were unable to access the All-Russia State Television and Radio Broadcasting Company website or the Smotrim live-streaming platform for periods during the speech.
The IT Army, a hacktivist group founded at the start of the war against Ukraine, was linked by a Twitter account to confirm the attack as malicious online actors.
description.17: A Russian hacking collective called KillNet claimed responsibility for disrupting NATO's cyberinfrastructure and stealing data in a series of DDoS attacks. Bypassing security measures, KillNet used stolen plain-text login credentials to create accounts on a gay dating portal in Kyiv and Moldova. Citing the group's tactics as a response to the ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine, NATO Secretary General Jens Stoltenberg said that the organization has deployed additional cyber defense measures to safeguard its sensitive data from such attacks.
description.18:
A U.S. authorities have seized 13 more domains linked to some of the world's most popular DDoS-for-hire websites.
These websites, also described as "booter" or "stressor" services, are marketed as legitimate security testing tools that allow admins to stress-test websites. In reality, the services are used for launching denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks designed to overwhelm websites and networks and force them offline.
The DOJ announced on Monday that the FBI had seized 13 more domains linked to some of the most prolific booter services as part of Operation PowerOFF, an international law enforcement effort to disrupt online platforms that allow anyone to launch massive distributed DDoS attacks.
description.19: A DDoS attack targeted several Italian websites and online services, including a major bank, due to an overwhelming influx of traffic that saturated their systems. The attack was likely carried out by Russian cybercriminals, according to expert Marcus Murray. The incident highlighted the vulnerability of digital infrastructure in Italy and prompted authorities to take swift action to block the malicious traffic.
description.20:
A DDoS attack targeted Akamai Technologies' website.
B Affected: Akamai Technologies, Inc., Radware, and several other companies.
C Key technical details: The attack was reported to be a Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attack, with the attackers using a combination of botnets and custom-made malware to overwhelm the targeted systems. The attack resulted in significant network congestion, leading to downtime for the affected companies. The incident highlights the growing threat posed by DDoS attacks on critical infrastructure and online services.
description.21:
A massive Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attack was launched against a customer in Asia-Pacific on February 23, 2023, peaking at 900.1 gigabits per second and 158.2 million packets per second.
The attack involved hundreds of thousands of computers flooding the targeted server with malicious traffic, which was then filtered out by Akamai's scrubbing network, a global data center system that uses more than 225 frontline responders to mitigate the attack.
Akamai's scrubbing network used its industry-leading combination of platform, people, and processes to pre-mitigate the assault without direct or collateral damage, with most attack traffic bursting during the peak minute of the attack.
description.22:
A DDoS attack targeted a major web hosting service provider, causing widespread disruptions to its customers' online services.
B Affected were thousands of websites and applications worldwide, including popular e-commerce platforms and social media sites.
C Key technical details revealed that the attack was launched from multiple locations around the globe, using a combination of botnets and malware.
description.23: A DDoS attack targeted several Italian government and infrastructure sites, including the Ministry of Defense, Carabinieri, BPER Bank, A2A Energy, and the Interior Ministry's Carta d'identità, in a response to Premier Meloni's visit to Kiev and Ucraina's military support. The attackers used distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks that exploited vulnerabilities in software to disrupt services. The incident highlights the growing threat of cyber terrorism against Italy, which is seen as a target by Russian-backed groups.
description.24:
A DDoS attack targeted the European Parliament website.
B The attack was described as a Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) event by multiple news sources.
C The affected websites were all part of the European Parliament's online presence, with some reporting significant traffic spikes and disruptions.
description.25:
A DDoS attack targeted the European Parliament website, causing service disruptions just moments after members voted to designate Russia a state sponsor of terrorism.
The attack was attributed to pro-Russian hackers who claimed responsibility on Twitter, and the site's availability was impacted from outside due to high levels of external network traffic.
Experts have warned that such attacks can cause significant problems for businesses and individuals, including disruption to online services and potential harm to democratic institutions.
description.26: A DDoS attack targeted the European Parliament's website, causing it to be inaccessible for several hours. The attack was attributed to a group called Killnet, which has claimed responsibility in the past for similar attacks on Russian targets. The incident highlighted Russia's alleged sponsorship of terrorism and its use of cyberattacks against Western countries.
description.27:
A DDoS attack targeted Sweden's parliament website, disrupting access to its web page and causing partial downtime on Tuesday and Wednesday.
The attack was attributed to a "distributed denial-of-service" (DDoS) attack by hacktivists, which works by directing high volumes of internet traffic towards targeted servers.
Sweden applied for NATO membership in the wake of Russia's invasion of Ukraine and authorities warned of increased cyber attacks against Swedish interests.
